commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Telegram and Discord model picker persistence to retain existing auth profile overrides during model-only selections and adds Discord regression tests for fallback and hidden-dispatch paths.

PR surface: Source +53, Tests +89. Total +142 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main's Telegram picker and Discord picker fallback call `applyModelOverrideToSessionEntry` without a profile or preserve flag, and the helper then clears existing auth override fields.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow mechanical repair on the PR branch: make picker auth preservation provider-aware and add cross-provider regression coverage.

target.title: fix(model-picker): preserve auth profile override when selecting a model
target.body:
## Summary

The native model pickers select a model only and never own an auth profile, yet
they were silently destroying an `authProfileOverride` established by a different
source (a CLI flag, a session-level override) on every model switch. The user
just sees "Model changed to X" with no warning that their auth profile override
was wiped.

Fixes #92244.

## Root cause

`applyModelOverrideToSessionEntry` (`src/sessions/model-overrides.ts`) takes the
destructive clear branch (`else if (!params.preserveAuthProfileOverride)`) and
deletes `authProfileOverride` / `authProfileOverrideSource` /
`authProfileOverrideCompactionCount` whenever a selection carries no
`profileOverride` and the caller does not opt out. The pickers select a model
only, so they always hit that branch.

## Fix (both picker surfaces)

- **Telegram** (`extensions/telegram/src/bot-handlers.runtime.ts`): the picker
  persists the override directly via `applyModelOverrideToSessionEntry`. It now
  passes `preserveAuthProfileOverride: true`, so a model-only selection no longer
  clears the override.

- **Discord** (`extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command-model-picker-apply.ts`):
  the picker's *primary* persistence path is the hidden `/model` dispatch, which
  routes through the shared directive code (`directive-handling.persist.ts`) and
  calls the helper without the flag — so passing the flag at the picker's own
  fallback write is not enough. Since the picker never owns an auth profile, it
  now **captures** the override before dispatch and **restores** it afterward (in
  `finally`), covering both the hidden-dispatch and direct-persist paths. This is
  contained to the Discord extension and does **not** change shared `/model`
  directive semantics for manually typed `/model` commands.

### Sibling surfaces

- `extensions/voice-call/src/response-generator.ts` calls the helper with
  `selectionSource: "auto"` for an automatic config-driven response model, not an
  interactive user picker. That is a different intent (no user-owned auth profile
  to protect), so it is intentionally out of scope.
- The shared directive path (`directive-handling.persist.ts` /
  `directive-handling.impl.ts`) intentionally manages the profile for manual
  `/model` directives and is left unchanged; the Discord fix preserves the
  picker's intent without altering that behavior.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: using a native model picker (Telegram or Discord) to switch
models no longer deletes an `authProfileOverride` that was set by a non-picker
source; the new model override is still applied.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout, driving the real
`applyModelOverrideToSessionEntry` helper and the real session-store
runtime (`loadSessionStore` / `patchSessionEntry` / `upsertSessionEntry`) against
a real on-disk session store file in a temp dir — the actual shipped code paths,
not mocks.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran a harness through the real
helpers via `node_modules/.bin/tsx` that seeds a CLI-sourced auth profile
override, then exercises each picker's persistence path and reads the persisted
store back. Console output below.

Evidence after fix: copied live console output (terminal capture) from the run:

```text
Session store: /var/folders/.../openclaw-proof-92244-fn9tGo/sessions.json

[0] seeded CLI auth profile override:            { model: '(none)', authProfileOverride: 'cli-profile', authProfileOverrideSource: 'user' }
[A] Telegram picker BEFORE fix (no flag):        { model: 'lmstudio/unsloth/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it@iq4_xs', authProfileOverride: '(deleted)', authProfileOverrideSource: '(deleted)' }
[B] Telegram picker AFTER fix (preserve flag):   { model: 'lmstudio/unsloth/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it@iq4_xs', authProfileOverride: 'cli-profile', authProfileOverrideSource: 'user' }
[C] Discord hidden /model dispatch (destroys):   { model: 'lmstudio/unsloth/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it@iq4_xs', authProfileOverride: '(deleted)', authProfileOverrideSource: '(deleted)' }
[D] Discord picker AFTER fix (capture+restore):  { model: 'lmstudio/unsloth/gemma-4-26b-a4b-it@iq4_xs', authProfileOverride: 'cli-profile', authProfileOverrideSource: 'user' }

(captured before dispatch: cli-profile)
```

Observed result after fix: line [A] reproduces the bug on the real helper (the
override is deleted); line [B] shows the Telegram fix preserving it; line [C]
shows why the flag alone cannot fix Discord (its hidden dispatch still clears the
override); line [D] shows the Discord capture+restore keeping the override intact
while the new model (`lmstudio/...`) is still applied.

What was not tested: a live end-to-end Telegram/Discord channel round-trip
through a running bot (no automated inline-picker callback harness and no live
bot credentials in this environment). The reported Telegram surface is the
one-line flag, and both surfaces are covered by the real-helper run above plus
the regression tests below.

## Tests

Added Discord regression tests in
`extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.model-picker.test.ts`:
- "preserves an auth profile override set by another source when picking a model"
  (fallback-persist path)
- "restores an auth profile override cleared by the hidden /model dispatch"
  (primary dispatch path)

Both fail on current `main` and pass with this change. Local: `node
scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.model-picker.test.ts`
→ 22 passed; `pnpm tsgo:extensions` + `pnpm tsgo:test:extensions` → exit 0;
`oxfmt --check` + `oxlint` on touched files → clean.
